findings.title: Previous-bid refund is an unguarded external call that enables both reentrancy theft and auction lockup

best_agent_match.title: Previous-bid refund is an unguarded external call that enables both theft-by-reentrancy and auction lockup

findings.title: `makeBid()` lacks auction-state gating, enabling premature settlement and invalid post-settlement bids

best_agent_match.title: Auction settlement can be triggered before the game ends

findings.title: Replacement bids only need to exceed 5% of the current bid, allowing the winning price to be ratcheted down to dust

best_agent_match.title: Outbids only need to exceed 5% of the current bid, so the winning price can be ratcheted downward toward dust

findings.title: Claims transfer token rewards to the zero address instead of the claimant

best_agent_match.title: Claims send token rewards to the zero address instead of the claimant

findings.title: Unchecked ERC20 return values can allow free writes and silent token-payout failures

best_agent_match.title: Unchecked ERC20 return values can allow free writes or silent token-claim failures

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can set malicious token to steal user funds
rejected_candidates.reason: This depends on the owner deliberately configuring a malicious external token; that is a trust/configuration assumption about the chosen asset, not a protocol bug in `Game.sol`.

rejected_candidates.title: Owner can set malicious NFT contract to steal NFT
rejected_candidates.reason: This is likewise an owner-chosen malicious dependency rather than an exploitable flaw in the game logic itself.

rejected_candidates.title: Incorrect share calculation causes incorrect ETH payouts
rejected_candidates.reason: `chunksWritenCount` tracks the number of chunks that have ever been initialized, and `_ownersShare` transfers one share from the old owner to the new owner on overwrite, so the sum of all live shares equals `chunksWritenCount`.

rejected_candidates.title: Sum of all claim shares does not equal total, funds locked
rejected_candidates.reason: For the same reason, the intended denominator is consistent with the live share total; only normal integer-division dust may remain, which is not a material vulnerability here.

rejected_candidates.title: No reentrancy guard on ether transfers
rejected_candidates.reason: The generic claim-side reentrancy concern is not substantiated by an exploit path beyond the specific refundable-bid reentrancy already captured in F-001; `claim()` sets `isClaimed` before sending ETH.

rejected_candidates.title: Empty input array causes unnecessary token transfer
rejected_candidates.reason: An empty `writeChunks()` call is at most a user footgun/gas waste and does not create realistic protocol-level harm.

rejected_candidates.title: No way to recover mistakenly sent ETH or tokens
rejected_candidates.reason: Missing rescue functionality for accidental transfers is not treated as a reportable vulnerability in this audit context.
